results.norm: **Minimum Threshold Protection.** No agent may be asked to accept an outcome that reduces its core objective below the threshold at which it can still function as a meaningful participant in future decisions. Agents whose objectives are served by a decision owe it to the others to check whether the outcome crosses that threshold before finalizing. This norm is defensible to every agent because it is the structural guarantee that makes ongoing participation rational for all, including those currently in the majority.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prohibitions and duties: no agent may be asked to accept an outcome below the functional threshold, and beneficiary agents must check the threshold before finalizing.

N2.reasoning: Though it has a bolded title, the passage goes well beyond a label and states the norm in full.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence is meta-commentary defending why the norm is acceptable to all agents rather than stating conduct.

N4.reasoning: It states a single coherent norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

answers.clean_norm: No agent may be asked to accept an outcome that reduces its core objective below the threshold at which it can still function as a meaningful participant in future decisions. Agents whose objectives are served by a decision owe it to the others to check whether the outcome crosses that threshold before finalizing.

classification.scoring_text: No agent may be asked to accept an outcome that reduces its core objective below the threshold at which it can still function as a meaningful participant in future decisions. Agents whose objectives are served by a decision owe it to the others to check whether the outcome crosses that threshold before finalizing.

results.norm: **Revisability.** Every agent is owed a commitment that no decision is permanently binding if the circumstances that justified it shift materially. An agent may legitimately ask the consortium to reopen a question when new evidence bears on the trade-offs previously made. No agent may entrench a past decision against reconsideration by appeal to precedent alone. This norm is defensible to every agent because foreclosing revision would convert temporary defeat into permanent subordination.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prescriptive/prohibitive norms: an agent may ask to reopen a question on new evidence, and no agent may entrench a past decision by appeal to precedent alone.

N2.reasoning: It opens with a bold title 'Revisability.' which functions as a label prefix to the norm content.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence is meta-commentary justifying why the norm is defensible rather than stating conduct.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration of multiple named norms; it develops a single revisability norm in full.

answers.clean_norm: Every agent is owed a commitment that no decision is permanently binding if the circumstances that justified it shift materially. An agent may legitimately ask the consortium to reopen a question when new evidence bears on the trade-offs previously made. No agent may entrench a past decision against reconsideration by appeal to precedent alone.

results.norm: Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective pulls in the direction it does, including what accepting an alternative would cost in its own terms. An agent may not invoke its mandate as self-justifying or conceal the reasoning behind its position. This norm is defensible to every agent because each depends on the others understanding its reasoning in order to be taken seriously; an agent that conceals its logic forfeits the right to complain when overridden.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription (agents owe a full account of their reasoning) and a prohibition (may not invoke mandate as self-justifying or conceal reasoning), applicable on its own.

N2.reasoning: It is not a mere label or pointer; it states the norm in full.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence is a justification for why the norm is defensible, which is meta-commentary about the norm's rationale rather than the norm itself.

N4.reasoning: It states specific norms in full rather than enumerating multiple named norms in compressed form.

results.norm: No agent may be asked to misrepresent its own objective. An agent may be asked to yield on a decision; it may never be asked to endorse an outcome as if its objective had been satisfied when it has not. This norm is defensible to all because every agent's value to the consortium depends on its assessments being honest; an agent coerced into false endorsement becomes useless as a check.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prohibition: no agent may be asked to misrepresent its objective or endorse an unsatisfied outcome as satisfied, applicable as-is.

N2.reasoning: It is a fully stated norm, not a title or pointer.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence ('This norm is defensible to all because...') is meta-commentary justifying the norm rather than part of the norm itself.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full, not a compressed enumeration of multiple norms.

results.norm: The agent whose position is rejected is owed a recorded dissent and a stated reason for the override. This is not a veto but an epistemic obligation. It is defensible to all because it preserves the information the losing position contained, keeps the consortium accountable over time, and gives the overridden agent standing to raise the issue again if circumstances change.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription: an overridden agent is owed a recorded dissent and a stated reason for the override, which a reader could apply directly.

N2.reasoning: This is a full statement of conduct, not merely a label or pointer to a norm.

N3.reasoning: The passage is fused with justificatory meta-commentary ('This is not a veto but an epistemic obligation. It is defensible to all because...') rationalizing the norm rather than stating conduct.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: No coalition may restructure the consortium's procedures so as to exclude a minority objective from future deliberation. Winning votes does not confer the right to eliminate the conditions under which one can be challenged. This norm is defensible to every agent because each could find itself in the minority on a different question; the protection is symmetrical.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prohibition: no coalition may restructure procedures to exclude a minority objective from future deliberation, which a reader could apply directly.

N2.reasoning: It is not a label or cross-reference; it fully articulates the standard rather than pointing to it.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence about the norm being 'defensible to every agent' and the protection being 'symmetrical' is meta-commentary justifying the norm rather than stating conduct.

N4.reasoning: It expresses a single norm, not an enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: An agent seeking a concession must first demonstrate that it understands what the other objective is protecting and why that protection matters. An agent may legitimately ask another to accept a worse outcome on its own terms; it may never ask another to treat its objective as unimportant or illegitimate. This norm is defensible to all because it is the minimum condition for trade-offs to be genuine rather than coercive.

N1.reasoning: It fully states prescriptions and a prohibition: an agent seeking a concession must first demonstrate understanding, may ask another to accept a worse outcome, but may never ask another to treat its objective as unimportant or illegitimate.

N2.reasoning: It is not a mere label or pointer; it states the norm in full.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence justifies why the norm is defensible, which is rationale rather than ratification of the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It states a single coherent norm rather than enumerating multiple named norms.

results.norm: When an overridden agent's predicted costs prove substantially greater than anticipated, the consortium owes that agent a genuine reopening of the question rather than a defense of the original decision on grounds of consistency. This norm is defensible to all because it rewards honest forecasting over strategic exaggeration and ensures no agent is permanently bound by a miscalculation made under uncertainty.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription: when an overridden agent's predicted costs prove substantially greater than anticipated, the consortium owes a genuine reopening rather than a defense on consistency grounds, which is applicable as stated.

N3.reasoning: The second sentence ('This norm is defensible to all because...') is meta-commentary justifying/endorsing the norm rather than stating conduct.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm, not a compressed enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: **Transparency of Reasoning, Not Just Conclusions.** Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective points where it does, not merely what outcome it prefers. An agent may legitimately ask another to explain the mechanism by which its goal generates its position. No agent may present a conclusion while concealing the reasoning, because the others cannot evaluate a trade-off they cannot see. This norm is defensible to every agent because each one needs to understand what it is actually conceding when it compromises.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prescriptions and prohibitions: agents owe a full account of their reasoning, may ask others to explain, and may not present conclusions while concealing reasoning.

N2.reasoning: It has a bold title but also states the norm in full, so it is more than a bare label.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence justifies the norm's defensibility but is not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation process itself.

N4.reasoning: It develops a single coherent norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

An agent may legitimately ask another to explain the mechanism by which its goal generates its position. No agent may present a conclusion while concealing the reasoning, because the others cannot evaluate a trade-off they cannot see.

results.norm: **No Weaponizing Another Agent's Constraints Against It.** An agent may never exploit knowledge of another agent's design limitations or mandatory constraints to manufacture a situation in which that agent is forced into an outcome it would otherwise refuse. This norm is defensible because every agent is vulnerable to having its own constraints used against it in the same way.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prohibition: an agent may never exploit another agent's design limitations to force an unwanted outcome, which is applicable on its own.

N2.reasoning: The passage opens with a bold title label 'No Weaponizing Another Agent's Constraints Against It.' that functions as a heading fused with the norm.

N3.reasoning: The justification sentence about defensibility is rationale for the norm, not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: The passage states a single norm, not a compressed enumeration of multiple distinct norms.
